title: Crataegus rotundifolia
taxonomy: Crataegus rotundifolia is classified as a species of hawthorn tree within the family Rosaceae. This family is commonly known as the rose family and consists of flowering plants, including trees, shrubs, and perennials. The genus Crataegus comprises over 200 species of hawthorn trees, many of which are native to the Northern Hemisphere. Crataegus rotundifolia is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, particularly in China, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, and Tajikistan. As a member of the Rosaceae family, Crataegus rotundifolia shares characteristics with other flowering plants in this family, such as showy white or pink flowers and small, fruiting branches.
uses: Crataegus rotundifolia is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various folk medicines for its potential health benefits. Its extracts have been studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's fruit, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Research suggests that the plant may have cardio-protective effects and be useful in managing diabetes due to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. However, more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
benefits: Crataegus rotundifolia, also known as the rounded-leaved hawthorn, has been used in traditional medicine for various health benefits. The plant is believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular-protective properties. It is thought to help lower blood pressure, improve lipid profiles, and reduce the risk of heart disease. The extracts of Crataegus rotundifolia have been studied for their potential to improve cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of certain cancers. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
properties: Crataegus rotundifolia, also known as the round-leaved hawthorn, is used in traditional medicine for its various therapeutic properties. The plant's berries and leaves are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits. Studies suggest that extracts from Crataegus rotundifolia may help lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exert anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the plant's antioxidant properties may also contribute to its potential anti-cancer and anti-aging effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Crataegus rotundifolia.

Crataegus rotundifolia, also known as the round-leaved hawthorn, contains various active constituents that contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant also contains oligomeric proanthocyanidin (OPC), which has been shown to have cardiovascular benefits and protect against oxidative stress. Additionally, Crataegus rotundifolia contains alkaloids, including crataegusine and crataegusic acid, which may have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. The specific composition and concentration of these active constituents can vary depending on the plant's growing conditions and geographical location.
parts: Crataegus rotundifolia is a species of hawthorn tree, and its medicinal parts are primarily the leaves, flowers, and fruits. The berries of the tree are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which contribute to their medicinal properties. The leaves and flowers of Crataegus rotundifolia are also used in traditional medicine due to their potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. The fruits of the tree are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The medicinal properties of Crataegus rotundifolia are still being researched, but it is often used in traditional medicine to support heart health and reduce stress.
preparations: Crataegus rotundifolia is known to be used in traditional medicine, with its herbal preparations often employed to address various health concerns. The plant's extracts are believed to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Herbal preparations from Crataegus rotundifolia are sometimes used to support cardiovascular health and may help lower cholesterol levels. In traditional medicine, the plant is also believed to possess antimicrobial properties, which could aid in wound healing and infection prevention. However, more research is necessary to fully understand the efficacy and potential risks associated with using Crataegus rotundifolia in herbal preparations.
intro: Crataegus rotundifolia, also known as hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Mediterranean region, valued for its medicinal and edible properties. The health benefits of hawthorn include lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and reducing the risk of heart failure, due to its ability to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow. The bioactive constituents of hawthorn include flavonoids, such as quercetin and anthocyanins, as well as triterpenoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Hawthorn is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, capsules, and extracts, often marketed for heart health and cardiovascular support.
